content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 12, 1993


Kenneth N. DeVoe
P.O. Box 133
Atwater, California  95301

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-93-094

Dear Mr. DeVoe:

     I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
March 8, 1993, wherein we discussed your request for advice,
Letter No. I-93-094 and your duties regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "PRA").
Since your letter does not seek advice regarding a specific
pending governmental decision and your question is general in
nature, we treat your letter as a request for informal assistance
pursuant to the provisions of Regulation 18329².  I also informed
you that we cannot provide advice on past conduct.

     You are a councilmember in the City of Atwater and also sit
on the city's redevelopment agency.  You are a property owner and
developer.  You state that you have disqualified yourself from
participating in many governmental decisions, but would like to
represent your personal interests, if possible.  We specifically
discussed Regulation 18700.1(b), (copy enclosed) which allows a
public official to appear in the same manner as any other member
of the general public before an agency to represent the official
interest in matters solely to the official's personal interests.
Since you and your wife wholly own the property and the business
that is developing the property, this regulation appears to be
applicable to your situation.


________________________
     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

     ² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA  95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-93-094
Page 2


     I informed you that pursuant to Regulation 18700.1, which
must be construed narrowly, as the owner/developer of the
property, you may appear as a member of the general public to
solely represent your own interests in a decision where you are
disqualified from participating in your official capacity.

     I trust this answers your question.  If you have any further
questions regarding this matter, you may contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Jeff Marschner
                                        General Counsel

                                        By:  Jill Stecher
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

JM:JS:dl
